# ARM Vectorization Correctness Checklist

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate_arm_intrinsics_manual.py. Do not edit manually. -->

这是 `validate-snippet` 的 quick reference 版本，便于在生成代码前先做人工 checklist。

## What This Checklist Covers

- `NEON`: 固定宽度循环、显式尾处理、禁止跨 ISA 混用
- `SVE`: 长度无关、谓词覆盖、头文件与 predicated load/store
- `SME`: streaming、ZA ownership、`svzero_za()` 和 ZA/tile 入口约束

## NEON

| Rule | What It Checks | Common Failure |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `NEON loops must keep an explicit scalar epilogue` | A fixed-width NEON loop must show how it handles the remainder elements. | Ending the kernel after the NEON loop with no scalar cleanup. |
| `Do not mix NEON, SVE and SME templates in one kernel body` | A generated kernel body should follow one SIMD model at a time: fixed-width NEON, scalable SVE or streaming/ZA SME. | Calling vld1q_f32 and svld1_f32 in the same loop body. |

## SVE

| Rule | What It Checks | Common Failure |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Do not mix NEON, SVE and SME templates in one kernel body` | A generated kernel body should follow one SIMD model at a time: fixed-width NEON, scalable SVE or streaming/ZA SME. | Calling vld1q_f32 and svld1_f32 in the same loop body. |
| `SVE and SME intrinsics must include the matching header` | sv* intrinsics require <arm_sve.h>; SME/ZA intrinsics and attributes require <arm_sme.h>. | Using svld1_f32 or svzero_za() with no Arm SVE or SME header include. |
| `SVE loads and stores must show predicate coverage` | Predicated loads and stores must use a live predicate argument, typically from svwhilelt_*. | Using svld1_* or svst1_* with no visible predicate generation. |
| `SVE loops must be vector-length agnostic` | The loop step must come from svcnt*() or an equivalent scalable-width construct. | Using i += 4 or another fixed lane count in an SVE loop. |

## SME

| Rule | What It Checks | Common Failure |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Do not mix NEON, SVE and SME templates in one kernel body` | A generated kernel body should follow one SIMD model at a time: fixed-width NEON, scalable SVE or streaming/ZA SME. | Calling vld1q_f32 and svld1_f32 in the same loop body. |
| `Do not fake SME ABI support routines with weak stubs` | Generated SME source must not define weak __arm_tpidr2_save, __arm_tpidr2_restore or __arm_za_disable stubs. | Adding weak stubs to make an executable link while silently disabling the real SME ABI contract. |
| `SME ZA inline asm must be standalone and scan-verifiable` | Standalone SME ZA inline asm must include explicit ZA state start/stop, fresh zeroing, outer-product accumulation and predicated write-back. | Returning success for inline asm that was not compiled, linked and scanned. |
| `SME intrinsics must appear in a streaming-capable context` | ZA and SME instruction intrinsics require a streaming-aware function context. | Calling svmopa_* or svadd_write_za* from a plain C function with no streaming attribute. |
| `svzero_za() requires fresh-ZA intent or explicit ZA output` | svzero_za() should only appear in a fresh-ZA path or a function that explicitly outputs ZA state. | Zeroing ZA in a kernel that should preserve or continue caller-owned accumulation. |
| `ZA intrinsics must declare ownership at the function boundary` | At least one ZA ownership attribute must be visible when a function uses ZA state. | Calling ZA intrinsics while omitting all of __arm_inout("za"), __arm_new("za") and __arm_out("za"). |
| `SVE and SME intrinsics must include the matching header` | sv* intrinsics require <arm_sve.h>; SME/ZA intrinsics and attributes require <arm_sme.h>. | Using svld1_f32 or svzero_za() with no Arm SVE or SME header include. |

## Rule Reference

### NEON

### `NEON loops must keep an explicit scalar epilogue`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_NEON`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: A fixed-width NEON loop must show how it handles the remainder elements.
- `Common Mistake`: Ending the kernel after the NEON loop with no scalar cleanup.
- `Example`:
```c
for (; i + 4 <= n; i += 4) { ... } for (; i < n; ++i) { ... }
```

- `Source`: [Arm Neon Intrinsics Reference](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/neon_intrinsics/advsimd.html) / Advanced SIMD (Neon) intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `Do not mix NEON, SVE and SME templates in one kernel body`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_NEON`, `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`, `__ARM_FEATURE_SME`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: A generated kernel body should follow one SIMD model at a time: fixed-width NEON, scalable SVE or streaming/ZA SME.
- `Common Mistake`: Calling vld1q_f32 and svld1_f32 in the same loop body.
- `Example`:
```c
Dispatch NEON, SVE and SME implementations in separate functions instead of mixing them inline.
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / Advanced SIMD intrinsics / SVE introduction / SME keyword attributes / 2026-05-12

### SVE

### `Do not mix NEON, SVE and SME templates in one kernel body`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_NEON`, `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`, `__ARM_FEATURE_SME`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: A generated kernel body should follow one SIMD model at a time: fixed-width NEON, scalable SVE or streaming/ZA SME.
- `Common Mistake`: Calling vld1q_f32 and svld1_f32 in the same loop body.
- `Example`:
```c
Dispatch NEON, SVE and SME implementations in separate functions instead of mixing them inline.
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / Advanced SIMD intrinsics / SVE introduction / SME keyword attributes / 2026-05-12

### `SVE and SME intrinsics must include the matching header`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`, `__ARM_FEATURE_SME`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: sv* intrinsics require <arm_sve.h>; SME/ZA intrinsics and attributes require <arm_sme.h>.
- `Common Mistake`: Using svld1_f32 or svzero_za() with no Arm SVE or SME header include.
- `Example`:
```c
#include <arm_sve.h>  // or <arm_sme.h> when SME/ZA intrinsics are used
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / Header files / <arm_sve.h> / <arm_sme.h> / 2026-05-12

### `SVE loads and stores must show predicate coverage`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: Predicated loads and stores must use a live predicate argument, typically from svwhilelt_*.
- `Common Mistake`: Using svld1_* or svst1_* with no visible predicate generation.
- `Example`:
```c
svbool_t pg = svwhilelt_b32(...); svld1_f32(pg, ...); svst1_f32(pg, ...);
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / Mapping of SVE instructions to intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `SVE loops must be vector-length agnostic`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: The loop step must come from svcnt*() or an equivalent scalable-width construct.
- `Common Mistake`: Using i += 4 or another fixed lane count in an SVE loop.
- `Example`:
```c
for (int i = 0; i < n; i += svcntw()) { ... }
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / SVE introduction / 2026-05-12

### SME

### `Do not mix NEON, SVE and SME templates in one kernel body`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_NEON`, `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`, `__ARM_FEATURE_SME`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: A generated kernel body should follow one SIMD model at a time: fixed-width NEON, scalable SVE or streaming/ZA SME.
- `Common Mistake`: Calling vld1q_f32 and svld1_f32 in the same loop body.
- `Example`:
```c
Dispatch NEON, SVE and SME implementations in separate functions instead of mixing them inline.
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / Advanced SIMD intrinsics / SVE introduction / SME keyword attributes / 2026-05-12

### `Do not fake SME ABI support routines with weak stubs`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SME`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: Generated SME source must not define weak __arm_tpidr2_save, __arm_tpidr2_restore or __arm_za_disable stubs.
- `Common Mistake`: Adding weak stubs to make an executable link while silently disabling the real SME ABI contract.
- `Example`:
```c
nm -u candidate.compare | rg '__arm_tpidr2|__arm_za_disable'
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SME ABI state and SME ZA state assertions / 2026-05-12

### `SME ZA inline asm must be standalone and scan-verifiable`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SME`
- `Required Attributes`: `__arm_streaming`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: Standalone SME ZA inline asm must include explicit ZA state start/stop, fresh zeroing, outer-product accumulation and predicated write-back.
- `Common Mistake`: Returning success for inline asm that was not compiled, linked and scanned.
- `Example`:
```c
__arm_streaming + smstart za + zero {za} + fmopa + predicated st1w + smstop za
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SME language extensions and intrinsics / Controlling the use of streaming mode / SME instruction intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `SME intrinsics must appear in a streaming-capable context`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SME`
- `Required Attributes`: `__arm_streaming`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: ZA and SME instruction intrinsics require a streaming-aware function context.
- `Common Mistake`: Calling svmopa_* or svadd_write_za* from a plain C function with no streaming attribute.
- `Example`:
```c
void kernel(...) __arm_streaming __arm_inout("za");
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / Header files / <arm_sme.h> / SME keyword attributes related to streaming mode / 2026-05-12

### `svzero_za() requires fresh-ZA intent or explicit ZA output`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SME`
- `Required Attributes`: `__arm_new("za")`, `__arm_out("za")`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: svzero_za() should only appear in a fresh-ZA path or a function that explicitly outputs ZA state.
- `Common Mistake`: Zeroing ZA in a kernel that should preserve or continue caller-owned accumulation.
- `Example`:
```c
void kernel(...) __arm_streaming __arm_new("za"); svzero_za();
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SME language extensions and intrinsics / SME instruction intrinsics / ZERO / 2026-05-12

### `ZA intrinsics must declare ownership at the function boundary`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SME`
- `Required Attributes`: `__arm_inout("za")`, `__arm_new("za")`, `__arm_out("za")`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: At least one ZA ownership attribute must be visible when a function uses ZA state.
- `Common Mistake`: Calling ZA intrinsics while omitting all of __arm_inout("za"), __arm_new("za") and __arm_out("za").
- `Example`:
```c
void kernel(...) __arm_streaming __arm_inout("za");
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SME language extensions and intrinsics / SME ZA state assertions / 2026-05-12

### `SVE and SME intrinsics must include the matching header`

- `Maps To`: `rule`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`, `__ARM_FEATURE_SME`
- `Use When`: 静态检查规则
- `Key Rule`: sv* intrinsics require <arm_sve.h>; SME/ZA intrinsics and attributes require <arm_sme.h>.
- `Common Mistake`: Using svld1_f32 or svzero_za() with no Arm SVE or SME header include.
- `Example`:
```c
#include <arm_sve.h>  // or <arm_sme.h> when SME/ZA intrinsics are used
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / Header files / <arm_sve.h> / <arm_sme.h> / 2026-05-12
